description: Typeform makes it smooth to build forms in my website, I will recommend for web developers and technical users
author: Samuel A.
pros: It help build technical forms in a very short time
cons: It's kind of complicated to use for someone without technical experience, especially the code embedding in source code

review_title: Steep learning curve, but worth the effort
description: It is a solid platform to do outward communication with large audiences. The learning curve is pretty high, but the integration with other platforms makes it worth it.
author: Damon B.
pros: The options are robust and the API integration with other platforms is helpful.
cons: The interface can be pretty confusing and the platform integration can break without warning.

review_title: An outstanding tool with room to grow for complex-volume forms
description: My overall experience with Typeform has been positive, especially due to its intuitive design, ease of use and ability to create visually appealing, engaging forms. It excels in providing a smooth, user-friendly experience for both creators and respondents. However, when working with a high volume of projects or complex workflows, certain limitations become apparent. The lack of advanced organizational tools, such as drag-and-drop functionality for duplicating projects across folders, can slow down productivity. I think something similar happen with the absence of bulk editing options for text or global settings for slides that can make repetitive tasks more tedious.
author: Gabriela N.
pros: It makes simple the creation of form with pre-made templates, renponsiveness and appealing layout.
cons: What I liked least about Typeform is the lack of more advanced drag-and-drop options, especially for duplicating projects across different folders. For those of us working with a high volume of projects, having this feature would save a lot of time and make organization much smoother. Additionally, it would be great to have more text-editing options, such as bulk find-and-replace for words or phrases, as well as more global settings that apply to all slides instead of adjusting each slide individually.

author: Lindsay B.
pros: I really love how Typorms’forms look clean and professionall, personnlized without typing any script code. It cost but the very worthful
cons: The only con for Typeform I would say is the pricing. The freen plan doent gives enouth and paid can get expensive

description: Typeform has a lovely UX and is easy to create surveys with answers that built off of customer response.
author: Melissa B.
pros: I love how easy it is to map custom journeys with Typeform. There is an excellent mapping tool to help plan different paths and dependencies.
cons: Typeform's reporting features aren't as robust as some of the competition, but I am still able to get the results I need.

description: Proven to be a game-changer for creating engaging, dynamic surveys. The standout feature, especially for researchers, is the ease with which you can implement logic jumps to reroute respondents through different paths based on their answers. This functionality allows for the creation of complex, tailored surveys that feel personal and relevant to each participant, enabling me to gather more nuanced and specific insights from different respondent segments. Typeform’s real-time data sharing via live links has been incredibly useful for keeping stakeholders informed while the surveys are still open. Stakeholders can check progress and view preliminary insights as they unfold, which promotes transparency and ensures continuous engagement throughout the research process.
author: Sarah P.
pros: Typeform excels in allowing you to add logic that reroutes respondents based on their answers. This is perfect for tailoring surveys to different audience segments or guiding participants through more complex decision trees without overwhelming them. It helps capture richer data by asking the right follow-up questions based on each user’s input. Real-Time Data Sharing: You can share live links to survey results while the survey is still active, allowing stakeholders to monitor responses as they come in. This real-time insight fosters early discussions and alignment on key findings while the data collection process is ongoing.
cons: Typeform provides some reporting and analytics, but for UX researchers needing deep analysis, exporting data to external tools like Google Sheets for further analysis is necessary.

description: So efficient, simplified interface and saves time
author: Kara S.
pros: Helps me with research and making surveys through quizzes and questionnaire all i do is , just describe the form i need and AI builds it which i can edit also to make some changes. Its AI features are so fantastic and make work easier
cons: Currently am not experiencing any problems using this software

review_title: Worth the money for short campaigns
description: It's one of those rare applications that are just fun to use like a Miro and Notion. When you discover it's part of your tech stack - you're excited. I love that.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Typeform is the best survey tool out there. It's easy to setup, fun to customize, and very enjoyable for users to fill out. I use it for technical challenges as well, and our applicants have mentioned they've enjoyed their experience as well.
cons: It's quite expensive, but it's worth the cost for short campaigns. I wouldn't commit to paying for this long-term when there's many other tools in your tech stack that you need to account for. There's many free options out there like Google Forms, or slightly cheaper tools like Survey Monkey.

description: I use typeform to screen new clients. It has helped tremendously because it minimizes the need for me to physically ask them the questions on the typeform. I can sort clients and review the data to help market.
author: latoya B.
pros: The ease of use. It's so user friendly and they update the software regularly.
cons: It could load a little faster. But in all honesty, it's an overall great software.

description: It is a user-friendly interface and its a AI-driven making it speedy to save time
author: Gisele F.
pros: It helps me in survey tools provisions like creation of online forms
cons: I haven't found any problem with this software

review_title: Typeform is one the most friendly, high-design experience platforms to build a interactive form
author: Madhura K.
pros: Typeform is one the most friendly, high-design experience platforms to build a variety of forms.
cons: There are a lot of design options but at times it becomes confusing to use the correct type of input response for the question.

review_title: Great product for integrations and with a lot of room for improvement
author: Elgiz A.
pros: It was very easy to setup an early lead gen with Typeform for my project. It also has a great UX and customizable enough to make it look native.
cons: It is costly therefore not the best as a long time solution, but amazing for any short term data collection.

author: Brian D.
pros: Like how the forms look really clean and professional without me needing to know any coding. People seem to enjoy filling them more cause of the way it flows one question at a time.
cons: No big issue for me honestly, works like it should and I get the responses I need so can’t complain much.

author: Ramos J.
pros: I really like the auto save feature that enables users to continue filling a form later. I also like the diverse question formats at our disposal when creating forms.
cons: There is room for improvement in the functionality of exporting and filtering data.

description: The form designs looks really good and it was easy to customize the form fields. Also gathering insights was fairly easy on the dashboard.
author: Babatunde O.
pros: By far the best feedback collection form I have ever integrated on my website was built with Typeform.
cons: For the plan that meets my capacity, i had to pay 46euro per month which is quite a lot of investment for a startup, so in general it is costly.

description: Overall, my experience is positive. We use this product to gauge customer feedback on our software
author: Leeav D.
pros: I loved the user interface and that their product was simple to navigate and implement.
cons: Sometimes I wanted to add choices and I didn’t have the option to.

description: Cut my teeth on Typeform years back, it's my go‑to gizmo for snagging client feedback with flair
author: Lucas H.
pros: Form builder packs themes galore, logic jumps, custom URLs. Auto‑save keeps flow breezy, tweak labels mid‑session, no wipe worry
cons: But the tier pricing pinches when hits spike and export paths feel slow if you're mining chunky stats

review_title: Great software if you're looking for a one-stop form creating and info capture platform
author: Benjamin C.
pros: Good free and paid platform and very simple to use.
cons: Paid platform is expensive but mostly worth it

description: I used it for quick reviews from all my clients and also when they need changes to my service offerings
author: Gregor F.
pros: It's fast to create forms and share them anywhere: website, email or social media
cons: The free subscription has always met all my needs

description: I use Typeform for creating responding forms for my different surveys and it makes everything easier
author: Madeleine B.
pros: It has a smart UI for easy navigation. I particularly like its templates for quick form creation and logic connection features which are lacking in most form builders
cons: The main drawback is that it doesn't offer anything substantial in the free plan

description: My overall experience is great. I can be creative with my forms.
author: Quantieshia B.
pros: I like that I can create different types of forms.
cons: I think everything about Typeform is good.

review_title: A little pricey, yet worth it
author: Lorenzo M.
pros: It has great feedback management, offers custom branding and integrates well with websites
cons: The free trial offers only 10 submissions per month, which is too little even for beginners
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Easy to use too that helps you to do simple research even with the free version.
author: Antti M.
pros: Easy to use UI, excellent tool for gathering feedback and having all the results in one place and well formatted.
cons: One could argue that the pricing is a bit off, since there is a lot of tools that can fulfill the same job.

review_title: Great for form collection with sharp presentation
description: If you don't recognize Typeform by its brand name, you would certainly recognize the product. Sleek, line-by-line forms. Many of our clients really like the product.
author: Kevin M.
pros: Survey response tech with very cool packaging. It makes your form feel sleek.
cons: A little expensive and limited for what it is.

review_title: Simple, but adequate with good integrations.
author: Allison S.
pros: It's a simple interface without an overwhelming number options and integrates well with Klaviyo.
cons: Would like to have more options for customizing the form layouts.
